evals.prompt: Define a TypeORM Post entity with an auto-increment primary key, a title column, and a many-to-one relation to a User entity (a User has many Posts). Include the explicit foreign key column.
evals.expected_output: A TypeScript entity file using TypeORM decorators where Post has a @PrimaryGeneratedColumn id, a @Column title, a @ManyToOne relation back to User, and User exposes the inverse @OneToMany posts collection.
assertions: Uses the @Entity() decorator on the Post class
Declares the relation with @ManyToOne(() => User, (user) => user.posts)
Declares the inverse side with @OneToMany(() => Post, (post) => post.author) on User
Includes an explicit foreign key column (e.g. authorId) and/or @JoinColumn
Uses @PrimaryGeneratedColumn() for the id

evals.prompt: Write a TypeORM migration that creates a users table with id, email (unique), and created_at columns, and drops the table on rollback.
evals.expected_output: A migration class implementing MigrationInterface with an up() that creates the users table and a down() that drops it.
assertions: Class implements MigrationInterface
Defines async up(queryRunner: QueryRunner) and async down(queryRunner: QueryRunner) methods
up() creates the table (e.g. queryRunner.createTable with a new Table)
down() drops the table (e.g. queryRunner.dropTable('users'))
Marks the email column as unique (isUnique: true)

evals.prompt: Using the TypeORM query builder, fetch active users together with their posts, ordered by user creation date descending, returning at most 10 rows.
evals.expected_output: A query that uses createQueryBuilder with a join to posts, a parameterized where clause, ordering, and a take/limit.
assertions: Calls createQueryBuilder('user')
Joins the posts relation with leftJoinAndSelect('user.posts', 'post')
Uses a parameterized where clause such as .where('user.isActive = :isActive', { isActive: true })
Orders results with .orderBy('user.createdAt', 'DESC')
Limits results with .take(10) and resolves with .getMany()

evals.prompt: Show how to create a User and a related Post inside a single TypeORM transaction so both are saved atomically.
evals.expected_output: Code wrapping both saves in a transaction, either via AppDataSource.transaction(manager => ...) or a QueryRunner with explicit commit/rollback.
assertions: Opens a transaction via AppDataSource.transaction(...) or queryRunner.startTransaction()
Saves both the User and the Post using the transaction's manager
Commits on success (commitTransaction or implicit via the transaction callback)
Handles failure with rollbackTransaction (when using QueryRunner) or relies on the callback's automatic rollback
Releases the QueryRunner in a finally block when the QueryRunner approach is used
